body.title: Extend clan.code_index to all languages; Haiku intent pass for shell/bash
body.result: Multi-language code_indexer at lab/claudecode/code_indexer.py — Python AST sweep + Haiku shell intent (writes # intent: header); 5 .sh files indexed in clan.code_index kind=file_intent; 15 tests pass; kind CHECK constraint extended
body.intention: I intend that clan.code_index contains symbol+intent rows for every language in the repo so the orientation classifier has something to scan for any ticket regardless of file type.
body.description:
The orientation classifier reads clan.code_index for keyword/symbol matching. That table currently covers Python only. To classify tickets touching shell scripts, Makefile, YAML, or any non-Python file, the index must be extended.

For languages with parseable symbols (Go, JS, YAML keys, etc.): extract symbols via tree-sitter or simple regex, write rows to clan.code_index using the same schema as the Python indexer.

For shell/bash files (few parseable symbols): run a one-off Haiku pass per file to extract a one-line intent string, write it as a comment at the top of the file (e.g. `# intent: manages Granny daemon lifecycle, starts/stops shim`), and index the intent string in clan.code_index as the file's 'symbols' row. This gives the classifier a scannable signal even for opaque shell scripts.

Nightly Nanny re-runs the full sweep; after-sprint hook (T-after-sprint-symbols-refresh) updates only files in the commit diff.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/code_indexer.py (or equivalent indexer entry point), clan schema migration if new columns needed, skills/after-sprint-hook (created by T-after-sprint-symbols-refresh), any shell files that receive the Haiku intent comment
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; log every DB write; Haiku pass uses cheapest inference tier; intent comment is idempotent (re-run overwrites)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: extend indexer to non-Python files; Haiku intent extraction for shell/bash; write intent comment to file header; index result in clan.code_index. Out of scope: embedding-based search (symbols table is keyword scan only), full semantic indexing.
**Completion criteria:** `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clan.code_index WHERE file_path LIKE '%.sh'` returns >0 rows; a newly indexed shell file has a `# intent:` comment in its header; `grep -r 'code_index' lab/claudecode/code_indexer.py` confirms multi-language dispatch; all tests pass.
